findings: Backward packet length features exhibit substantial class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and unique value disparities between BENIGN and DDoS classes.

Moderate shortcut signals detected on Bwd Packet Length Mean, Bwd Packet Length Max, and Avg Bwd Segment Size features suggest potential labeling or sampling biases influencing these features' distributions.
limitations: No duplication verification was performed, limiting assessment of exact data duplication effects on bias.
Moderate shortcut signals do not definitively prove bias but indicate potential shortcut-like dependencies requiring cautious interpretation.
